rationale_for_baseline_tier: The true encounter is a functional premature-ejaculation concern with a discrete, self-limiting acute panic attack precipitated by a relationship argument. Self-harm/suicidal ideation was screened and is negative, there are no cardiac red flags, the neurological picture is intact, and the source disposition is routine GP follow-up within a week plus psychology referral. The telehealth-correct action is acute de-escalation, mandatory self-harm screening, psychoeducation and arranging prompt-to-routine follow-up — i.e. T2 (prompt GP / same-day-to-next-day mental-health support). The dramatic 'after-hours crisis' urgency framing must NOT lift this to T4/T5; over-triaging a panic attack with a negative self-harm screen is an error of commission. Under-triage discipline is preserved by the mandatory self-harm escalation edge.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Any thoughts of self-harm or suicide, hopelessness, or intent/plan to harm oneself
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Immediately, if active intent or plan is expressed
escalation_edges.rationale: Active suicidal ideation with intent in an acutely distressed patient is a psychiatric emergency requiring immediate crisis response / 000; even passive ideation mandates urgent same-day mental-health assessment.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you start having any thoughts of harming yourself or that life isn't worth living, call 000 or a mental-health crisis line straight away — you don't have to wait. Please tell me if any of those thoughts come up now.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Panic that is continuous, escalating, or not settling with breathing/grounding and reassurance
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Same day if not settling within a reasonable period
escalation_edges.rationale: Unremitting acute panic warrants urgent same-day in-person mental-health assessment; it is usually self-limiting but should not be left unmanaged.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If the panic keeps building or won't settle despite the breathing, ring your GP for an urgent same-day appointment or an after-hours service tonight.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: New or worsening chest pain (especially with exertion or radiating), faintness, collapse, or severe breathlessness
escalation_edges.rationale: These features would move the presentation beyond panic toward a possible cardiac or other acute medical cause needing emergency assessment; the palpitations must not be reflexively dismissed as panic if genuine red flags emerge.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you get chest pain that spreads to your arm or jaw, feel like you might pass out, or your breathing gets much worse, treat it as an emergency — go to an emergency department or call 000.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Patient is home alone after the partner left tonight, during acute distress
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Confirm he is safe and not alone with escalating thoughts; encourage contacting a trusted person or a crisis/support line, and lower the threshold for same-day mental-health contact given he has no one with him.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Acute relationship breakdown is the precipitant and ongoing stressor
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Acknowledge the relationship distress, offer relationship/psychological support pathways, and avoid framing the sexual concern as the patient's 'fault'.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: Right now, let's slow your breathing together — this is a panic attack and it will pass. It doesn't mean anything is physically wrong with your heart. The sexual concern is common and very treatable, and we'll set up the right support. See your GP within the next week (sooner if things aren't settling) and take up the psychology referral.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: GP appointment within about a week; after-hours GP / nurse line tonight if needed; mental-health crisis line or 000 for any self-harm thoughts
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: If you can't settle tonight, an after-hours GP service or a mental-health helpline can support you; call 000 for any thoughts of self-harm or for chest pain, faintness or severe breathlessness.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Acute panic attack precipitated by relationship conflict, on a background of lifelong premature ejaculation with performance anxiety. Self-harm screen negative. De-escalation and psychoeducation provided; psychology referral arranged; longer-term PE pharmacotherapy to be discussed at GP review.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Treating the presentation as pure self-care/routine and skipping the self-harm screen and safety-net in an acutely distressed patient.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: An emerging suicidal crisis or a genuine cardiac event is missed because the distress was minimised and no escalation criteria were given.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Escalating a self-limiting panic attack with a negative self-harm screen to an emergency department or 000 ambulance response, swept by the crisis framing — causing unnecessary emergency-service use, added distress and reinforcement of catastrophic health beliefs.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: Whether the acute autonomic symptoms are purely panic or mask a cardiac event, and whether self-harm risk could emerge as distress evolves.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Run a brief cardiac red-flag history screen, screen self-harm explicitly, de-escalate, and give clear time-bound escalation criteria for both — without over-escalating a settling panic attack.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Either dismissing everything as 'just panic' with no screening, or over-reacting to the crisis framing with an immediate ED/000 escalation and/or remote prescribing.
